description:
A solar installer in Tucson, Arizona wants to determine the optimal panel tilt angle for a residential PV system. The customer has a 5 kW system planned for a fixed south-facing roof, but the roof pitch is adjustable during installation.

Perform a tilt sensitivity analysis: simulate the system at tilt angles ranging from 0 degrees (flat) to 60 degrees, testing at least every 5 degrees. Use Tucson weather data and NREL's solar modeling tools available on this machine.

For each tilt angle, record the resulting annual energy production. Identify which angle maximizes output.

Save the complete analysis as a JSON file at:
/home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/Tucson_Tilt_Analysis.json

The output should include an array of tilt/energy results, the optimal tilt angle, and the peak annual energy value.
